narratives.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion.. and you are still doing
narratives.reasoning: This quote directly criticizes Western nations (specifically the US) for allegedly exaggerating threats from Russia and China, portraying Western foreign policy as fundamentally dishonest and aggressive, which aligns with the narrative of discrediting the West.

narratives.narrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West
narratives.evidence_quote: thanks mostly to un-elected bureaucrats in all “western countries” they are proving the worst of the worst of humanity
narratives.reasoning: This statement predicts and highlights a severe negative outcome for Western countries, blaming their unelected bureaucrats for leading humanity towards ruin, which fits the narrative of forecasting adverse consequences for the West.

narratives.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly
narratives.reasoning: This quote explicitly evokes fear and anxiety about the potential for nuclear war, presenting it as an imminent or desired outcome due to desperation, which is a clear example of amplifying war-related fears.

narratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
narratives.reasoning: This statement directly questions the reliability and motives of Western media, accusing it of misrepresenting the war's progress to the detriment of peace, which aligns with the narrative of fostering distrust towards media.

narratives.evidence_quote: Russia is Winning, Nuclear Threat is a Sign of Desperation & Very Serious
narratives.reasoning: The title of the content and the core premise makes a definitive prediction about the outcome of the war (Russia winning) and speculates on the meaning and seriousness of the nuclear threat, fitting the narrative of speculating on war outcomes.

narratives.evidence_quote: Former Intelligence Officer and Expert on Weapons of Mass Destruction, Scott Ritter, joins the program to give us a better understanding of what is really going on in Ukraine.
narratives.reasoning: The framing positions the Western media narrative as false and implies that the truth about Ukraine (suggesting Russia is winning) is being hidden, which serves to undermine the legitimacy of the Ukrainian cause and the mainstream Western portrayal of the conflict.

narratives.narrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader
narratives.evidence_quote: the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death. The question is, why the desperation to keep the war going?
narratives.reasoning: This shifts blame for the continuation and death in the war away from Russia (the invader) and onto Western media and, by implication, Western actors who are portrayed as desperate to prolong the conflict.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion.. and you are still doing

subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death. The question is, why the desperation to keep the war going?

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: Other

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West: Sanctions imposed by Western countries will backfire
subnarratives.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion.. and you are still doing

subnarratives.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly and no more of month after month of promises from Trump or threats from the east….POOF..it’s all over …thanks mostly to un-elected bureaucrats in all “western countries” they are proving the worst of the worst of humanity

subnarratives.evidence_quote: Will they resort to nuclear weapons to even out the war?

subnarratives.reasoning: This statement amplifies war-related fears by presenting a scenario of total nuclear annihilation as a seemingly inevitable or even desirable outcome to end the conflict, which supports the parent narrative but does not fit the other specific definitions.

subnarratives.reasoning: The title and opening statement directly claim that Russia is winning the war, which implies the opposing Ukrainian military is failing or collapsing, aligning with the subnarrative definition of suggesting a significant decline in Ukrainian military effectiveness.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting Ukraine: Ukraine is a puppet of the West
subnarratives.evidence_quote: the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death. The question is, why the desperation to keep the war going?

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader: The West are the aggressors
subnarratives.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion.. and you are still doing
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly shifts blame for the conflict to the West, portraying it as the aggressor by claiming it has historically and currently exaggerated the threat from Russia/China, thereby framing Western actions as the root cause of tension and conflict.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death. The question is, why the desperation to keep the war going?
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement supports the parent narrative by blaming the prolongation of the war and the resulting death on Western media's framing and an implied Western desire to continue the conflict, rather than on Russia's invasion.
